zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Boson於1240年出生在英國英格蘭戴文時，他的父親William de Boson41歲，母親Mrs. Margery De Boson35歲。他於1257年與Joan de Saint-George在英國英格蘭戴文卡素 希爾結婚。他去世於1285年在英國英格蘭戴文普林普頓 聖 瑪麗享年45歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Boson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Hungarian: probably from a derivative of a personal name like Boda or Bodó, Hungarian pet forms of some Slavic name such as Budimir, Budislav (see Buda 3).

Polish (Bodoń); Slovak (rarely Bodoň): derivative of the Polish verb bość (1 sg. bodę), Slovak bodať ‘to gore, to prick’, or from the German personal name Bodo . In Poland, the bearers of this surname live almost exclusively in Czarnków-Trzcianka County in Greater Poland Voivodeship.
Spanish (Bodón): topographic name from bodón ‘winter pond or pool’, or a habitational name from El Bodón (Salamanca), a place called with this word.

variants.name: Buda
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Rocco, Caesar, Enrico, Lorenzo, Sal.

Italian: from a Late Latin name buda for a kind of rush, probably used as a metonymic occupational name for a maker of articles made of such rushes. In Sicily, it could be from bbuda ‘stem of a spike’.
Italian: habitational name from either of two places called Buda.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Boson was born in 1240, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Boson, was 41 and his mother, Mrs. Margery De Boson, was 35. He married Joan de Saint-George in 1257, in Castle Hill, Devon, England, United Kingdom. He died in 1285, in Plympton St Mary, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 45, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.Plympton St Mary, Devon, England: Plympton St Mary, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Boson est né en 1240, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Boson, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Margery De Boson, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Saint-George en 1257, à Castle Hill, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé en 1285, à Plympton St Mary, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ru.Plympton St Mary, Devon, England: плимптон ст мэри, девон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Boson родился в 1240 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de Boson было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Margery De Boson, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Saint-George в 1257 г. в местности кастл хилл, девон, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер в 1285 г. в местности плимптон ст мэри, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 45 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия, Великобритания.
